description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base constructed from large, roughly hewn gray blocks, some of which are covered in green moss or algae. The well is topped with a pitched roof made of reddish-brown clay tiles, which are weathered and uneven, giving a rustic appearance. Two thick wooden posts support the roof, and these posts also show signs of age and moss growth. A simple metal crank handle is attached to one side, suggesting a mechanism for drawing water, though no bucket or rope is visible. The interior of the well is open and dark, indicating depth, and the structure is robust and solid, likely intended for outdoor use in parks or courtyards. The overall design is utilitarian with no visible decorative elements, focusing on function and durability.
description: A rustic stone water well with a mossy circular base, wooden supports, and a weathered clay-tiled roof. It features a metal crank handle and an open, deep interior.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pitched clay-tiled roof, mossy wooden supports, and the metal crank handle on the right side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full width of the roof, the open well interior, and the crank handle extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear look at the roof's ridge and the mossy wooden support structure, no crank handle visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the roof, open well, and the crank handle now on the left side.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A metal crank handle is present on the side, but no pulley or bucket is visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high and a pitched roof supported by wooden posts.
